INGV DEM Mt Etna.title: Digital Elevation Model of Mt Etna updated to 18 December 2015
INGV DEM Mt Etna.servicedescription: OGS WMS provided by Osservatorio Etneo
INGV DEM Mt Etna.datadescription: Ganci G., Cappello A., Bilotta G., Hérault, A., Zago V., Del Negro C. (2019). Digital Elevation Model of Mt Etna updated to 18 December 2015, supplement to: Ganci, Gaetana; Cappello, Annalisa; Bilotta, Giuseppe; Hérault, Alexis; Zago, Vito; Del Negro, Ciro (2018): Mapping Volcanic Deposits of the 2011–2015 Etna Eruptive Events Using Satellite Remote Sensing. Frontiers in Earth Science, 6:83 [Data set]. PANGAEA - Data Publisher for Earth & Environmental Science. https://doi.pangaea.de/10.1594/PANGAEA.899140
INGV DEM Mt Etna.dataprovider: Istituto Nazionale di Geofisica e Vulcanologia (INGV)
publications: Ganci, G., Cappello, A., Bilotta, G., Herault, A., Zago, V., Del Negro, C. (2018, June 21). Mapping Volcanic Deposits of the 2011–2015 Etna Eruptive Events Using Satellite Remote Sensing. Frontiers in Earth Science. Frontiers Media SA. https://doi.org/10.3389/feart.2018.00083

Ganci, G., Cappello, A., Zago, V., Bilotta, G., Herault, A., Del Negro, C. (2018, September 11). 3D Lava flow mapping of the 17–25 May 2016 Etna eruption using tri-stereo optical satellite data. Annals of Geophysics. Instituto Nazionale di Geofisica e Vulcanologia, INGV. https://doi.org/10.4401/ag-7875

INGV TINITALY.title:
TINITALY, a digital elevation model of Italy with a 10 meters cell size (Version 1.1)

Style NameDescription
tinitaly_demTINItaly DEM
tinitaly_hshdShaded relief map
tinitaly_hsvHue-Saturation-Value (HSV) composition image with slope (2.5σ clipping) as saturation and SVF (2.5σ clipping) as value, according to RRIM (Red Relief Image Maps) style maps
tinitaly_odOpenness down map using equal area equalization as technique for image enhancement
tinitaly_slopeSlope map using 2.5σ clipping as technique for image enhancement
tinitaly_svf_2d5  Sky view Factor (SVF) map using 2.5σ clipping as technique for image enhancement
tinitaly_svf_eaSVF map using equal area equalization as technique for image enhancement
INGV TINITALY.datadescription: Tarquini S., Isola I., Favalli M., Battistini A., Dotta G. (2023). TINITALY, a digital elevation model of Italy with a 10 meters cell size (Version 1.1). Istituto Nazionale di Geofisica e Vulcanologia (INGV). https://doi.org/10.13127/tinitaly/1.1
INGV TINITALY.dataprovider: Istituto Nazionale di Geofisica e Vulcanologia (INGV)
publications: S. Tarquini, I. Isola, M. Favalli, F. Mazzarini, M. Bisson, M. T. Pareschi, E. Boschi. (2009). TINITALY/01: a new Triangular Irregular Network of Italy [JD]. Annals of Geophysics, 50(3). https://doi.org/10.4401/ag-4424

Tarquini, S., Vinci, S., Favalli, M., Doumaz, F., Fornaciai, A., & Nannipieri, L. (2012). Release of a 10-m-resolution DEM for the Italian territory: Comparison with global-coverage DEMs and anaglyph-mode exploration via the web. Computers & Geosciences, 38(1), 168–170. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cageo.2011.04.018

Tarquini, S., & Nannipieri, L. (2017). The 10 m-resolution TINITALY DEM as a trans-disciplinary basis for the analysis of the Italian territory: Current trends and new perspectives. Geomorphology, 281, 108–115. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.geomorph.2016.12.022
